question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user induce a network partition that causes the parse(List<T>) batch loop to call shouldParse() with a previous value that was set during the current batch (not from the database), and when the partition causes a mid-batch failure, the next retry re-reads getLast() from the database which returns a different value, causing the hash chain check to use a different expectedHash than the one used in the failed attempt, potentially accepting a file that should have been rejected?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a network partition that causes the BlockFileSource.doGet() to throw a BlockStreamException wrapping a timeout, and the AbstractBlockSource retry logic to re-download the same block number, but the discoverNetwork() lazy getter has already cached a stale network folder name, causing the re-download to fetch a file from a different (post-reset) network and pass it to onEnd() with a mismatched hash chain?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that makes the Downloader.verifySignatures() catch a HashMismatchException and log a warning (not an error), then continue to the next signature in the loop, and if all CONSENSUS_REACHED signatures fail hash verification due to the partition-induced stale lastStreamFile, the method returns false and the stream file is silently skipped, causing onEnd() to never be called and creating a permanent gap in the mirror node's data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes the executorService in BatchStreamFileNotifier to be shut down (via close()) while the StreamFileSubscriber.run() loop is blocked on queue.poll(), causing the InterruptedException handler to return without processing remaining items in the queue, so that onEnd() is never called for buffered stream files and those files are permanently lost?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a network partition that causes the flush() method in BatchStreamFileNotifier to evaluate lag = DomainUtils.now() - streamFile.getConsensusEnd() as a very large value (because the partition delayed processing), causing the window check to always return false and the buffer to grow unboundedly until maxFiles or maxItems is reached, then flushing a massive batch to onEnd() that causes an OOM or transaction timeout?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that interrupts the AbstractStreamFileParser.parse(T) method after doParse() succeeds but before doFlush() is called, and since the @Transactional annotation wraps the entire parse() method, the transaction is rolled back, but the in-memory last AtomicReference is not reset, causing the next parse() call to use the stale last value and skip the hash chain re-verification against the database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user induce a network partition that causes the RecordFileParser.doParse() to publish a RecordFileParsedEvent via applicationEventPublisher before onEnd() is called, and if the event listener performs database operations that succeed while the outer transaction (containing onEnd()) is rolled back due to the partition, the event-driven side effects are committed but the stream file record is not, creating an irreconcilable inconsistency?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a network partition that causes the streamFileRepository.findLatest() call inside getLast() to throw a DataAccessException, which propagates up through shouldParse() and parse(), causing the entire parse cycle to fail with an unchecked exception that is not an ImporterException, bypassing any ImporterException-specific error handling in callers of onEnd()?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that makes the BlockStreamVerifier.verifySignature() fail after verifyHashChain() has already mutated the blockFile object (e.g., setPreviousHash() or setPreviousWrappedRecordBlockHash() called in the cutover path), leaving the blockFile in a partially-modified state that is then retried with incorrect hash values, causing onEnd() to eventually receive a file with corrupted hash fields?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes the CompositeRecordStreamFileListener.onEnd() to throw an ImporterException from the i-th listener, leaving listeners at indices i+1 through n-1 un-notified, and since the exception propagates to doFlush() which does not catch it, last.set(streamFile) is never called, causing the next parse cycle to re-process the same stream file and call the first i listeners again, resulting in duplicate processing for those listeners?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: onEnd(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a network partition that causes the RecordFileParser.parse() @Transactional method to time out (transactionTimeoutString exceeded), and since the timeout causes a TransactionTimedOutException (a RuntimeException), the @Retryable annotation retries the entire parse including doParse() and onEnd(), potentially causing duplicate event publishing via applicationEventPublisher.publishEvent() for the same RecordFile?
